name: E2E: every axis-scoped rule is tagged using only canonical four-axis vocabulary drawn from the Linguist / IaC / PURL authority mirrors — no rule invents a language, dialect, or package-type kind
command: ruby -ryaml -rjson -rset -e 'ling=JSON.parse(File.read(ARGV[1])); known=Set.new; src=ling.is_a?(Hash) ? ling : {}; (src["languages"]||src).each{|k,v| known<<k.to_s.downcase; (v.is_a?(Hash) ? Array(v["aliases"]) : []).each{|a| known<<a.to_s.downcase}}; (src["by_extension"]||{}).each{|e,ls| Array(ls).each{|l| known<<l.to_s.downcase}}; r=YAML.unsafe_load_file(ARGV[2]); iac=(r["iac_dialects"]||{}).keys.map(&:downcase).to_set; purl=(r["purl_uses"]||{}).keys.map(&:downcase).to_set; bad=[]; scoped=0; Dir[File.join(ARGV[0],"generated-code-quality-standards","*","*.yaml")].each{|f| d=(YAML.unsafe_load_file(f) rescue nil); next unless d.is_a?(Hash); (d["rules"]||[]).each{|ru| at=ru["applies_to"]; next unless at.is_a?(Hash); scoped+=1; Array(at["linguist_aliases"]).each{|v| bad<<"#{ru["id"]}:ling:#{v}" unless known.include?(v.to_s.downcase)}; Array(at["iac_dialects"]).each{|v| bad<<"#{ru["id"]}:iac:#{v}" unless iac.include?(v.to_s.downcase)}; Array(at["purl_uses"]).each{|v| bad<<"#{ru["id"]}:purl:#{v}" unless purl.include?(v.to_s.downcase)}}}; STDERR.puts("axis_scoped=#{scoped} invented=#{bad.size} #{bad.first(5).inspect}"); exit(bad.empty? ? 0 : 1)' "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT" "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/vendor/canonical-vocabulary/linguist-languages.json" "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/standards/kind-to-tool-routing.yaml" && echo every-rule-vocab-canonical 1>&2
